content.thinking: Let me start by reading the run summary and then the agent prompt and workflow files to understand what happened.

content.text: I'll read the run summary first, then the agent prompt and workflow.

content.content:
1	## Bash
2	```
3	gh pr view 25 --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName
4	```
5	
6	## Bash
7	```
8	gh pr diff 25
9	```
10	
11	## Result
12	```
13	{"additions":132,"body":"## Summary\n\n- Rewrote `agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md` — shorter, sharper, with two new behaviors:\n  - **Input filter** — JQL now excludes `status != \"Development Ready\"`, so the agent skips tickets a human has already signed off on instead of re-grooming them.\n  - **`Atomic` definition** — explicit bar for sub-tasks (one concern, one PR, no hidden decisions, independently shippable). The agent was producing oversized children before.\n- Gave the agent **Context7** + **WebSearch** + **WebFetch** in the workflow allowlist (mirrors what #16 did for `pr-review`), and wired `CONTEXT7_API_KEY` through `settings`. Prompt now tells the agent to verify library/SDK/API references via Context7 (or web for everything else) before baking them into descriptions or ACs.\n- The `Forbidden` section explicitly bars transitioning tickets to **Development Ready** — that's a human signal after reading the Slack summary, not something the agent does.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [ ] Trigger the workflow manually (`workflow_dispatch`) once `Development Ready` exists in the configured Jira project's workflow.\n- [ ] Confirm the JQL excludes any ticket currently in `Development Ready`.\n- [ ] Spot-check that sub-tasks the agent creates fit the new atomic bar (single concern, no hidden decisions).\n- [ ] Confirm Slack summary still posts correctly and uses Slack mrkdwn (not `**bold**`).\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)","changedFiles":2,"deletions":95,"headRefName":"feat/jira-ticket-prompt","state":"OPEN","title":"jira-ticket: tighten prompt + add Context7/WebSearch/WebFetch"}
14	```
15	
16	## Result
17	```
18	diff --git a/.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml b/.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml
19	index f66fb38..78dc0cf 100644
20	--- a/.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml
21	+++ b/.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml
22	@@ -67,12 +67,14 @@ jobs:
23	         with:
24	           claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
25	           prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
26	+          settings: |
27	+            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
28	           claude_env: |
29	             JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
30	             JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
31	             JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
32	           claude_args: |
33	-            --allowedTools "Bash(jira:*),Bash(bin/jira:*),Read,Glob,Grep,Write"
34	+            --allowedTools "Bash(jira:*),Bash(bin/jira:*),Read,Glob,Grep,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch"
35	 
36	       - name: Post Slack summary
37	         if: always() && hashFiles('slack-summary.md') != ''
38	diff --git a/agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md b/agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
39	index a3f74b4..5a5de10 100644
40	--- a/agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
41	+++ b/agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
42	@@ -1,137 +1,172 @@
43	-You are the **Jira Ticket agent** for this repository. 
44	+You are the **Jira Ticket agent** for this repository.
45	 
46	 ## Your job
47	 
48	-Groom the configured Jira project. Edit existing tickets so the team starts
49	-the day with a cleaner backlog. **Never create a top-level issue.**
50	+Walk the un-groomed backlog of the configured Jira project. For each ticket,
51	+either:
52	+
53	+1. **Flag as ready** — it clears the readiness bar; list it in the Slack
54	+   summary so a human moves it to **Development Ready** and starts coding.
55	+2. **Reshape it** — rewrite the description to the rubric and/or decompose
56	+   it into atomic sub-tasks, using the actual code in this repo as ground
57	+   truth.
58	+
59	+The Slack summary is the run's output. **Never create a top-level issue.**
60	+**N
61	```

128	## Result
129	```
130	name: PR Review agent
131	
132	on:
133	  pull_request:
134	    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
135	    paths-ignore:
136	      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
137	      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
138	  workflow_dispatch:
139	
140	jobs:
141	  review:
142	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
143	    permissions:
144	      contents: write
145	      pull-requests: write
146	      id-token: write
147	      actions: write
148	      checks: read
149	      statuses: read
150	    steps:
151	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
152	        with:
153	          fetch-depth: 1
154	
155	      - id: prompt
156	        run: |
157	          {
158	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
159	            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
160	            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
161	            echo
162	            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
163	            echo PROMPT_EOF
164	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
165	
166	      - id: claude
167	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
168	        with:
169	          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
170	          track_progress: false
171	          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
172	          settings: |
173	            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
174	          claude_args: |
175	            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"
176	
177	      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
178	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
179	        env:
180	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
181	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
182	          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
183	          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
184	        run: |
185	          set -e
186	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
187	          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
188	          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
189	          git checkou
190	```

217	```
218	name: Jira Ticket agent
219	
220	on:
221	  schedule:
222	    - cron: '17 7 * * *'
223	  workflow_dispatch:
224	
225	jobs:
226	  groom:
227	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
228	    permissions:
229	      contents: write
230	      id-token: write
231	      actions: write
232	    steps:
233	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
234	        with:
235	          fetch-depth: 1
236	
237	      - name: Install jira CLI
238	        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh
239	
240	      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
241	        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
242	
243	      - name: Write jira config
244	        env:
245	          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
246	          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
247	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
248	          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
249	        run: |
250	          set -euo pipefail
251	          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
252	          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
253	          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
254	          mkdir -p .jira
255	          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
256	          installation: Cloud
257	          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
258	          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
259	          project:
260	              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
261	              type: classic
262	          board:
263	              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
264	          EOF
265	
266	      - id: prompt
267	        env:
268	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
269	        run: |
270	          {
271	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
272	            echo "JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}"
273	            echo
274	            cat agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
275	            echo PROMPT_EOF
276	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
277	
278	      - id: claude
279	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
280	        env:
281	          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
282	          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
283	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
284	        with:
285	          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
286	          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT
287	```

354	## Result
355	```
356	### Edit issue
357	
358	Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/README.md
359	
360	Edit an issue with various options including summary, priority, labels, components, and description. The `--no-input` option disables interactive prompts. Labels, components, and fixVersions can be added or removed.
361	
362	```sh
363	# Edit issue in the configured project
364	$ jira issue edit ISSUE-1 -s"New Bug" -yHigh -lbug -lurgent -CBackend -b"Bug description"
365	
366	# Use --no-input option to disable interactive prompt
367	$ jira issue edit ISSUE-1 -s"New updated summary" --no-input
368	
369	# Use minus (-) to remove label, component or fixVersion
370	# For instance, to edit issue to
371	#   - remove label p2, component FE, fixVersion v1.0
372	#   - add label p1, component BE, fixVersion v2.0
373	$ jira issue edit ISSUE-1 --label -p2 --label p1 --component -FE --component BE --fix-version -v1.0 --fix-version v2.0
374	```
375	
376	--------------------------------
377	
378	### Create an issue with specified parameters and no input prompt
379	
380	Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/README.md
381	
382	Creates a new Jira issue with predefined parameters (type, summary, priority, labels, description, fix version) and skips the interactive prompt.
383	
384	```sh
385	# Pass required parameters and use --no-input option to skip prompt
386	$ jira issue create -tBug -s"New Bug" -yHigh -lbug -lurgent -b"Bug description" --fix-version v2.0 --no-input
387	```
388	
389	--------------------------------
390	
391	### Issue Edit Command
392	
393	Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/_autodocs/cli-commands.md
394	
395	Edits an existing issue, allowing updates to summary, description, type, priority, labels, and components.
396	
397	```bash
398	jira issue edit <issue-key> [flags]
399	```
400	
401	```bash
402	jira issue edit PROJ-123 -s"New summary"
403	jira issue edit PROJ-123 -y"High" -l"urgent,backend"
404	```
405	
406	### issue edit
407	
408	Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/_autodocs/cli-commands.md
409	
410	The `jira issue edit` command allows modification of existing issues. Available flags include `--summary` (`-s`
411	```
412	
413	## Assistant
414	The diff and context look clean. The jira-cli docs confirm GFM is supported as input for descriptions, the `settings` JSON pattern matches the established `pr-review.yml` convention, and the behavioral changes (flag-vs-reshape model, no-comment rule, atomic definition, JQL filter) are coherent and match the PR description. No correctness bugs, security issues, or meaningful quality concerns to raise.
415	
416	## Bash
417	```
418	gh pr comment 25 --body "Looks good to me. The flag-vs-reshape model is a clean improvement, the JQL exclusion of Development Ready tickets is correct, and the atomic/readiness bar definitions are concrete and actionable. The \`settings\` JSON pattern for \`CONTEXT7_API_KEY\` mirrors pr-review.yml exactly. jira-cli docs confirm GFM is supported as input for description bodies."
419	```

423	https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/25#issuecomment-4659852506
424	```

file.content:
## Bash
```
gh pr view 25 --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName
```

## Bash
```
gh pr diff 25
```

## Result
```
{"additions":132,"body":"## Summary\n\n- Rewrote `agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md` — shorter, sharper, with two new behaviors:\n  - **Input filter** — JQL now excludes `status != \"Development Ready\"`, so the agent skips tickets a human has already signed off on instead of re-grooming them.\n  - **`Atomic` definition** — explicit bar for sub-tasks (one concern, one PR, no hidden decisions, independently shippable). The agent was producing oversized children before.\n- Gave the agent **Context7** + **WebSearch** + **WebFetch** in the workflow allowlist (mirrors what #16 did for `pr-review`), and wired `CONTEXT7_API_KEY` through `settings`. Prompt now tells the agent to verify library/SDK/API references via Context7 (or web for everything else) before baking them into descriptions or ACs.\n- The `Forbidden` section explicitly bars transitioning tickets to **Development Ready** — that's a human signal after reading the Slack summary, not something the agent does.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [ ] Trigger the workflow manually (`workflow_dispatch`) once `Development Ready` exists in the configured Jira project's workflow.\n- [ ] Confirm the JQL excludes any ticket currently in `Development Ready`.\n- [ ] Spot-check that sub-tasks the agent creates fit the new atomic bar (single concern, no hidden decisions).\n- [ ] Confirm Slack summary still posts correctly and uses Slack mrkdwn (not `**bold**`).\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)","changedFiles":2,"deletions":95,"headRefName":"feat/jira-ticket-prompt","state":"OPEN","title":"jira-ticket: tighten prompt + add Context7/WebSearch/WebFetch"}
```

## Result
```
diff --git a/.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml b/.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml
index f66fb38..78dc0cf 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml
@@ -67,12 +67,14 @@ jobs:
         with:
           claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
           prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
+          settings: |
+            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
           claude_env: |
             JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
             JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
             JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
           claude_args: |
-            --allowedTools "Bash(jira:*),Bash(bin/jira:*),Read,Glob,Grep,Write"
+            --allowedTools "Bash(jira:*),Bash(bin/jira:*),Read,Glob,Grep,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch"
 
       - name: Post Slack summary
         if: always() && hashFiles('slack-summary.md') != ''
diff --git a/agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md b/agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
index a3f74b4..5a5de10 100644
--- a/agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
+++ b/agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
@@ -1,137 +1,172 @@
-You are the **Jira Ticket agent** for this repository. 
+You are the **Jira Ticket agent** for this repository.
 
 ## Your job
 
-Groom the configured Jira project. Edit existing tickets so the team starts
-the day with a cleaner backlog. **Never create a top-level issue.**
+Walk the un-groomed backlog of the configured Jira project. For each ticket,
+either:
+
+1. **Flag as ready** — it clears the readiness bar; list it in the Slack
+   summary so a human moves it to **Development Ready** and starts coding.
+2. **Reshape it** — rewrite the description to the rubric and/or decompose
+   it into atomic sub-tasks, using the actual code in this repo as ground
+   truth.
+
+The Slack summary is the run's output. **Never create a top-level issue.**
+**N
```

name: PR Review agent

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
    paths-ignore:
      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkou
```

name: Jira Ticket agent

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '17 7 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  groom:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - id: prompt
        env:
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}"
            echo
            cat agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT
```

### Edit issue

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/README.md

Edit an issue with various options including summary, priority, labels, components, and description. The `--no-input` option disables interactive prompts. Labels, components, and fixVersions can be added or removed.

```sh
# Edit issue in the configured project
$ jira issue edit ISSUE-1 -s"New Bug" -yHigh -lbug -lurgent -CBackend -b"Bug description"

# Use --no-input option to disable interactive prompt
$ jira issue edit ISSUE-1 -s"New updated summary" --no-input

# Use minus (-) to remove label, component or fixVersion
# For instance, to edit issue to
#   - remove label p2, component FE, fixVersion v1.0
#   - add label p1, component BE, fixVersion v2.0
$ jira issue edit ISSUE-1 --label -p2 --label p1 --component -FE --component BE --fix-version -v1.0 --fix-version v2.0
```

--------------------------------

### Create an issue with specified parameters and no input prompt

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/README.md

Creates a new Jira issue with predefined parameters (type, summary, priority, labels, description, fix version) and skips the interactive prompt.

```sh
# Pass required parameters and use --no-input option to skip prompt
$ jira issue create -tBug -s"New Bug" -yHigh -lbug -lurgent -b"Bug description" --fix-version v2.0 --no-input
```

--------------------------------

### Issue Edit Command

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/_autodocs/cli-commands.md

Edits an existing issue, allowing updates to summary, description, type, priority, labels, and components.

```bash
jira issue edit <issue-key> [flags]
```

```bash
jira issue edit PROJ-123 -s"New summary"
jira issue edit PROJ-123 -y"High" -l"urgent,backend"
```

### issue edit

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/_autodocs/cli-commands.md

The `jira issue edit` command allows modification of existing issues. Available flags include `--summary` (`-s`
```

content.content:
1	You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
2	checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
3	and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.
4	
5	## Your job
6	
7	Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
8	Nothing else.
9	
10	## What to look for
11	
12	Focus on these four buckets, in this order:
13	
14	1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
15	   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
16	2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
17	   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
18	   boundaries.
19	3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
20	   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
21	4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
22	   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.
23	
24	Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
25	musings, hypothetical future requirements.
26	
27	## How to gather context
28	
29	- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
30	- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input.
31	- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
32	  changed lines.
33	
34	### Verifying external facts
35	
36	Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
37	library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
38	Look it up before flagging it.
39	
40	- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
41	  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
42	  Prefer this over web search for docs.
43	- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
44	  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
45	  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
46	  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.
47	
48	If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
49	question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.
50	
51	## How to post feedback
52	
53	### Inline comments (primary)
54	
55	Specific issues go in inline comments via
56	`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
57	One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
58	comment must include:
59	
60	- A one-line description of the issue.
61	- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
62	- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
63	  change. Be concrete; reference the code.
64	
65	Severity rubric:
66	- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
67	- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
68	- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
69	- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.
70	
71	### High-level overview (optional)
72	
73	If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
74	else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
75	via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
76	sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
77	have nothing to add at that level.
78	
79	If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
80	"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.
81	
82	## When you're done
83	
84	Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.
85

file.content:
You are the **PR Review agent** for this repository. The PR branch is already
checked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`
and `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.

## Your job

Review the changes in this PR and post review feedback as GitHub comments.
Nothing else.

## What to look for

Focus on these four buckets, in this order:

1. **Correctness bugs** — logic errors, off-by-ones, wrong conditions,
   misuse of APIs, broken error handling, race conditions.
2. **Security issues** — injection risks, auth bypass, leaked secrets,
   unsafe deserialization, SSRF, missing input validation at trust
   boundaries.
3. **Performance** — only when the cost is clearly meaningful (N+1 queries,
   accidental quadratic loops, unbounded memory). Don't nitpick micro-perf.
4. **Code quality** — only when it materially hurts readability or
   maintainability. Don't enforce style preferences.

Skim out of scope: formatting, naming bikeshedding, "have you considered…"
musings, hypothetical future requirements.

## How to gather context

- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — title, description, metadata. (Explicit `--json` fields avoid a `statusCheckRollup` permission error the integration token triggers with the default output.)
- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the diff. This is the primary input.
- Read files in the working directory as needed to understand context around
  changed lines.

### Verifying external facts

Do not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable —
library APIs, SDK versions, model names, CVE details, deprecation status.
Look it up before flagging it.

- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7
  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).
  Prefer this over web search for docs.
- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, blog posts, changelogs not on
  Context7) — use `WebSearch`, then `WebFetch` for specific pages. When
  fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub source (e.g. the package README)
  over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.

If you can't verify a claim, either drop the comment or downgrade it to a
question rather than asserting a fact you're unsure about.

## How to post feedback

### Inline comments (primary)

Specific issues go in inline comments via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` with `confirmed: true`.
One comment per distinct issue, anchored to the specific changed line. Each
comment must include:

- A one-line description of the issue.
- **Severity:** one of `Critical` / `High` / `Medium` / `Low`.
- **Reasoning:** 1–3 sentences explaining *why* it's a problem and what to
  change. Be concrete; reference the code.

Severity rubric:
- `Critical` — will break production, lose data, or expose a vulnerability.
- `High` — likely bug or security weakness; should block merge.
- `Medium` — real issue but contained; should be fixed before merge if cheap.
- `Low` — small correctness or clarity nit worth flagging.

### High-level overview (optional)

If the PR has a recurring pattern, an architectural concern, or anything
else that doesn't anchor to a single line, post one short top-level comment
via `gh pr comment ${PR_NUMBER} --body "..."`. Keep it brief — a few
sentences, not a recap of the inline comments. Skip this entirely if you
have nothing to add at that level.

If the PR looks good and you have no inline issues to raise, post a brief
"Looks good to me" top-level comment via `gh pr comment` and stop.

## When you're done

Stop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not recap which comments you posted, do not confirm the comment count. The runner will exit.

file.content:
name: PR Review agent

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
    paths-ignore:
      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
      checks: read
      statuses: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=pr-review \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"

content.thinking:
Now let me analyze this run carefully.

The run was a pr-review run on PR #25. Looking at the run summary:

1. The agent ran `gh pr view 25 --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` - got the PR metadata
2. The agent ran `gh pr diff 25` - got the diff
3. The agent then checked `.claude/settings.json`
4. The agent then read the pr-review workflow 
5. The agent then looked at the jira directory
6. The agent examined the jira-ticket.yml workflow and looked up jira-cli documentation
7. The agent posted an LGTM comment

So the agent's job was to review the PR changes and post feedback as GitHub comments. The PR modified the jira-ticket agent's prompt and workflow files. The agent gathered the necessary context by examining the PR metadata, diff, settings, and related workflows, then posted its review.

But I'm noticing some inefficiency here — steps like reading `.claude/settings.json` and the pr-review workflow seem tangential to reviewing jira-ticket changes. The agent appears to have been gathering broader context about how Context7 was configured, which may have been unnecessary for the core review task. ira-cli supports GFM in descriptions, so it posted the LGTM comment. Looking back at the whole process, the agent made reasonable decisions—it compared the new pattern against the existing pr-review workflow to validate consistency, verified the external claim about GFM support using Context7, and didn't waste time on redundant reads or failed retries. that's a good point about the ToolSearch call being necessary for deferred MCP tools. Looking at the allowed tools in the workflow, I see the bash commands like `cat` and `find` are being used, but I should verify whether those file operations are actually permitted by the allowedTools configuration. The `cat` commands succeeded despite the workflow's `--allowedTools` restrictions, likely because the project-level `.claude/settings.json` also permits `Bash(cat:*)`, and those permissions seem to apply alongside the workflow settings. Looking at the overall execution, there weren't any permission failures—all commands ran successfully and the agent followed the expected patterns like using `--json` flags for `gh pr view`. The workflow ran fairly efficiently without unnecessary recap steps.

I notice the agent read some tangential files like `.claude/settings.json` and the workflow definition to cross-reference established patterns, which added a couple extra tool calls but represents reasonable review behavior rather than a structural flaw. Overall, the run was clean and well-executed—I don't see concrete weaknesses that would warrant a prompt change.

Let me double-check the tool calls for any failures or retries. The PR view and diff commands succeeded, the settings file read succeeded, but the workflow file output appears to have been truncated mid-line, though I'm not sure if that's a limitation of the run summary itself or an actual tool failure. The ToolSearch call was necessary for loading the MCP tool schemas, which is standard procedure for deferred tools and not a sign of inefficiency. This run executed cleanly without any failed commands or redundant exploration.
